SEVP Innovation Lab + Knowledge Management AI MVP

Building federal AI capability that compounds — from one MVP to an enduring institutional advantage

Slide 1 of 8

SEVP Innovation Lab + Knowledge Management AI MVP

Building federal AI capability that compounds — from one MVP to an enduring institutional advantage.

Prepared for ICE/SEVP Executive Leadership. Prepared by Varun Malhotra, President — Changeis, Inc., in partnership with Perplexity Computer. April 2026.

Source: Title slide.
Slide 2 of 8

A focused 12-week MVP can convert SEVP institutional knowledge into a compounding AI capability — at $450–650K and FedRAMP-High by design.

Three columns: where SEVP is today, what we propose, and what the agency gets in 90 days.

Where we are: knowledge fragmented across SharePoint/Confluence/JIRA; attrition risk to institutional memory; long onboarding ramps. What we're proposing: 12-week MVP on AWS GovCloud + Bedrock (Claude Sonnet 4.5); three live KM use cases; FedRAMP High by design; eval-first with HITL guardrails. What it delivers: 25–40% time saved on search & synthesis; ~30% faster onboarding; reusable Innovation Lab playbook; clear ATO and Year-1 scale path (~$1.5–2.0M).

Source: Changeis SEVP Innovation Lab MVP proposal (2026-04-23); Anthropic FedRAMP High program (2025); GSA OneGov Anthropic deal (2025).  ·  Changeis SEVP MVP proposal  ·  Anthropic FedRAMP High  ·  GSA OneGov / Anthropic
Slide 3 of 8

Quantified value: 25–40% knowledge-worker time back, 30% faster onboarding, and a reusable lab that compounds across SEVP.

Conservative-to-upside ranges based on McKinsey, Gartner and GAO benchmarks for federal generative-AI deployments.

Knowledge workers spend ~20% of their week searching for or synthesizing information (McKinsey Global Institute). Federal generative-AI use cases grew 9× in one year — 32 → 282 across 11 agencies (GAO 2025). Gartner forecasts 60% of agencies will use AI agents for >50% of citizen transactions by 2029. Rough sizing: 200 SEVP knowledge-workers × 25% × 6 hrs/week × $90/hr ≈ $1.4M/year recovered capacity — before onboarding, retention, and risk-reduction value.

Source: McKinsey State of AI 2025; McKinsey Global Institute; GAO-25-107653; Gartner Public Sector AI; Anthropic + GSA OneGov; Changeis analysis.  ·  McKinsey State of AI 2025  ·  McKinsey Generative AI economic potential  ·  GAO-25-107653 federal AI use  ·  Gartner public sector AI
Slide 4 of 8

Federal and enterprise precedents have already proven this model — SEVP can move with high confidence, not from a standing start.

Five reference deployments across federal innovation labs, applied AI sprints, and enterprise knowledge management.

GSA TTS Centers of Excellence — federal IT-modernization CoE driving reusable AI playbooks across DoL/DHS. VA AI Tech Sprint — cohort-based 12-week sprints with industry, $1M prize pool, scaling winners inside VHA. DoD CDAO + Anthropic — $200M ceiling agreement for Claude on FedRAMP-High infrastructure (validates our reference architecture). Anthropic + GSA OneGov — Claude for Government (FedRAMP High) and Claude for Enterprise to all three branches for $1 for one year (Aug 2025). JPMorgan COIN — enterprise KM precedent saving ~360,000 hours/year on legal document review.

Source: GSA TTS CoE; VA AI Tech Sprint; CRS DoD CDAO realignment (Sep 2025); Anthropic + GSA OneGov (Aug 2025); JPMorgan COIN public reporting.  ·  GSA TTS Centers of Excellence  ·  VA AI Tech Sprint  ·  CRS DoD CDAO realignment  ·  Anthropic OneGov / Claude for Government  ·  JPMorgan COIN coverage
Slide 5 of 8

The MVP is delivered in three 4-week phases with parallel use-case tracks, gated reviews, and a Demo Day at Week 12.

Discovery & Lab stand-up → Build & Pilot → Validate & Scale; mid-point Go/No-Go at Week 6.

Phase 1 (Weeks 1–4): stand up the Innovation Lab; governance and eval harness; corpus discovery for three parallel use cases. Phase 2 (Weeks 5–8): RAG build + golden eval set; summarization + citation chain; case-officer drafting prototype with human-in-the-loop. Phase 3 (Weeks 9–12): pilot, measure accuracy and adoption; user-test with redaction & guardrails; Demo Day + scale plan. Stakeholder rhythm: weekly check-ins; bi-weekly steering. Eval gates at Weeks 4, 6, 8, 12.

Source: Changeis SEVP Innovation Lab MVP proposal (2026-04-23); NIST AI RMF (Govern–Map–Measure–Manage); Changeis delivery methodology.  ·  Changeis SEVP MVP proposal  ·  NIST AI RMF 1.0
Slide 6 of 8

The reference architecture is FedRAMP-High by design: AWS GovCloud + Bedrock (Claude Sonnet 4.5) + RAG + CAC/PIV identity + audit.

One page, no surprises — every component already has a federal precedent and an ATO path.

Inside the FedRAMP-High boundary on AWS GovCloud (US): user & identity (CAC/PIV via SAML; role-based access control); application & orchestration (institutional Q&A, policy/SOP synthesis, case-officer copilot); AI models, RAG & data (Amazon Bedrock; Claude Sonnet 4.5 via Claude for Government; Bedrock KB / OpenSearch retrieval; SharePoint, Confluence, JIRA, Study-in-the-State as sources); cross-cutting controls (audit & logging, PII redaction & DLP, eval harness, KMS encryption FIPS 140-2). Compliance pillar: FedRAMP High, DoD IL4/IL5, ATO path, Section 508, OMB M-25-21 / M-25-22, NIST AI RMF.

Source: Anthropic Claude for Government (FedRAMP High); AWS GovCloud documentation (IL4/IL5); OMB M-25-21 (Apr 2025); OMB M-25-22 (Apr 2025); NIST AI RMF 1.0.  ·  Anthropic Claude for Government (FedRAMP High)  ·  OMB M-25-21 / M-25-22 summary  ·  NIST AI RMF 1.0
Slide 7 of 8

Six discipline-grade practices we will apply on day one — drawn from NIST AI RMF, OMB guidance, and Changeis delivery experience.

Each practice is observable, measurable, and tied to a specific deliverable.

1) Human-in-the-loop by default — every high-impact output reviewed before action. 2) Eval-first development — golden datasets and acceptance thresholds written before code ships. 3) Privacy-by-design — automated PII detection & redaction, role-based retrieval, DLP inside the FedRAMP boundary. 4) Reuse-or-buy posture — Bedrock primitives, GSA OneGov Claude pricing, existing IdP. 5) Continuous ATO via DevSecOps — security artifacts and SBOMs generated by the pipeline. 6) Outcome metrics from day 1 — time saved, accuracy, adoption, SME satisfaction instrumented before launch.

Source: NIST AI RMF 1.0; OMB M-25-21 & M-25-22 (Apr 2025) replacing M-24-10 / M-24-18; McKinsey State of AI 2025; Changeis delivery practice.  ·  NIST AI RMF 1.0  ·  OMB M-25-21 / M-25-22 summary  ·  McKinsey State of AI 2025
Slide 8 of 8

Greenlight the 12-week MVP today — Week-6 Go/No-Go protects the investment, and Year-1 production scale follows on demonstrated value.

Three concrete asks to begin within the next two weeks.

Investment ladder: (a) Greenlight 12-week MVP at $450–650K labor + $8–15K/mo compute. (b) Mid-point Go/No-Go gate at Week 6 — eval gate on accuracy, adoption, ATO progress. (c) Year 1 production scale post-MVP at ~$1.5–2.0M with three use cases hardened and ATO. The asks: (1) Sponsor designated; (2) Knowledge SMEs identified (4–6 SEVP staff); (3) Initial use case selected for Week-1 kickoff. Contact: Varun Malhotra, President — Changeis, Inc. (varun@changeis.com).

Source: Changeis SEVP Innovation Lab MVP proposal (2026-04-23); McKinsey State of AI 2025; Anthropic + GSA OneGov (2025); Changeis ROM model.  ·  Changeis SEVP MVP proposal  ·  McKinsey State of AI 2025

high performers redesign workflows around AI - GAO-25-107653 (Jul 2025): federal generative-AI use cases grew 9× in one year (32 → 282 across 11 agencies) - Gartner: by 2029, 60% of government agencies will use AI agents for >50% of citizen transactions; 70% will require explainable AI + HITL - Anthropic + GSA OneGov (Aug 2025): Claude for Government (FedRAMP High) and Claude for Enterprise made available to all three branches for $1 for one year — eliminates acquisition friction - DoD CDAO selected Anthropic Claude under a $200M ceiling agreement — direct precedent for the proposed reference architecture - OMB now M-25-21 (Apr 2025) Accelerating Federal Use of AI and M-25-22 (Apr 2025) Driving Efficient Acquisition of AI replaced Biden-era M-24-10/M-24-18; deck cites the current-administration memos - Reference architecture (AWS GovCloud + Bedrock + Claude Sonnet 4.5 + RAG + CAC/PIV) inherits FedRAMP High from existing authorizations DEAD ENDS: - Could not find a single canonical $-figure for federal AI ROI; used range-based sizing tied to McKinsey time-savings + a transparent loaded-rate calculation - OMB memo numbering: confirmed M-24-10/M-24-18 are superseded by M-25-21/M-25-22 — deck cites both the new memos directly and notes the supersession in the source line on Slide 7 ASSUMPTIONS: - Pilot user base ≈ 200 SEVP knowledge workers at $90/hr loaded rate (illustrative ROM only) - 12-week duration and $450–650K labor ROM consistent with parent proposal - Year-1 production scale ROM ($1.5–2.0M) is directional; refined post-MVP based on adoption data OPEN QUESTIONS: - Final SEVP executive sponsor name (left as "TBD" / placeholder in The Ask) - Specific use case priority for Week-1 kickoff (institutional Q&A vs. policy synthesis vs. case-officer copilot) - 4–6 SME names for golden-dataset curation - Whether to expand from 8-slide lean version to 14-slide depth version DELIVERABLES: - /home/user/workspace/VM2-P-Taskers/sevp-executive-presentation-2026-04-25.pptx (8 slides, 16:9, ~370KB) - /home/user/workspace/VM2-P-Taskers/sevp-executive-presentation-2026-04-25.html (self-contained companion with embedded slide images) - portal.html updated with two new rows - Pushed to GitHub;
